transcript: So you might be wondering, how did I get here and here? Well, let's rewind. I was dared by Knorr to try something different for the good of the planet. We started the day by driving to a secret forest where we and fellow foodies had zero idea what was in store for us. We were greeted by our host where we were dared to experience the truth. I went first and was told to put on the first string and, well, I was splashed with the reality. The truth revealed that it can take over 20,000 liters of water to produce one kilogram of meat, but it takes 2,000 times less water to produce one kilogram of cricket protein. I was then dared to try crickets. This isn't my first time trying crickets, but after knowing the truth, I was even more excited to try. The cricket was crispy, savory, and paired with pad thai, super delicious. With quinoa, daring can be delicious. Stay tuned for my next video where I attend my first ever BYOB dinner party. Bring your own bug.
